state.output:
# Marketing Hub — All-in-One Marketing Command Center

> ⚡ **Core Doctrine: Intensity Beats Extensity — Every Time.**
> One channel mastered beats five channels touched. One offer sharpened beats ten offers scattered. One persona deeply understood beats five personas guessed. Read the full principle in [`docs/INTENSITY_PRINCIPLE.md`](docs/INTENSITY_PRINCIPLE.md).

A complete marketing platform built with **Next.js 14, TypeScript, Tailwind, and SQLite**. **30 interconnected sections** covering the entire marketing workflow — all running locally, no external API keys required.

## Quick start

```bash
cd ~/marketing-hub
npm install
npm run seed               # populate with 200+ sample records
npm run dev                # http://localhost:3000
```

For production:
```bash
npm run build
npm start
```

## What's inside (30 sections)

### 🧭 Plan
| Section | What it does |
|---|---|
| **Dashboard** | Unified metrics, module grid, traffic/conversion charts, active campaigns, upcoming content |
| **Brand kit** | Logos (primary/dark/icon), color palette, typography, voice & tone, usage guidelines |
| **Personas** | Audience profiles with goals, pain points, channels, messaging. Primary / secondary / anti-persona tiers |
| **Segments** | Rule-based audience segments (e.g. `score > 70 AND tag = newsletter`) |
| **Competitors** | Track competitors: positioning, pricing, SWOT analysis, strengths & weaknesses |
| **Campaigns** | Kanban (draft → scheduled → live → paused → completed), full CRUD, detail page, budget tracking |
| **Funnels** | Visual customer-journey builder — map awareness → landing → form → email → sale. Tracks conversion per step |
| **Budget planner** | Monthly budget by category, planned vs actual, variance tracking, category breakdown chart |
| **Playbooks** | Repeatable SOPs with steps, owners, durations, KPIs. Acquisition / activation / retention / revenue / branding |

### ✏️ Create
| Section | What it does |
|---|---|
| **Content calendar** | Monthly grid view with per-channel color coding, click-to-create |
| **Social studio** | Composer with per-channel character limits (Twitter 280, LinkedIn 3000, etc.), preview, scheduling |
| **Email builder** | Block-based editor (hero, features, CTA, testimonials, pricing, FAQ), live preview, HTML/text export |
| **Landing pages** | Same block editor for marketing pages, live preview, SEO meta, static HTML export |
| **Forms** | Drag-style form builder — text/email/phone/textarea/select/checkbox/number/URL fields, submit tracking |
| **Lead magnets** | Library of eBooks, templates, checklists, webinars, coupons, videos — track downloads |
| **Copy generator** | Offline template-based: headlines, hooks, CTAs, ad copy, social posts, email sequences, blog outlines |
| **Asset library** | File upload, image/video/document previews, search, tags |

### 📈 Grow
| Section | What it does |
|---|---|
| **SEO toolkit** | Keyword tracker (volume/difficulty/opportunity), HTML auditor, sitemap.xml, robots.txt |
| **Ads manager** | Multi-channel (Meta/Google/TikTok/LinkedIn), spend/impressions/clicks/CTR/CPA, pause/resume |
| **Events & webinars** | Webinars, conferences, meetups, workshops, launches — registration tracking, capacity fill rate |
| **CRM & deals** | Contacts with scoring, companies, deals kanban (drag between stages), activity tracking |
| **Automations** | Trigger→action rule builder (8 triggers × 8 actions), run tracking, pause/resume |
| **A/B testing** | Experiments with two variants, traffic & conversions, auto-computed lift & confidence, winner flag |
| **Testimonials** | Social proof library with ratings, sources (G2/Trustpilot/Twitter/email/manual), featured toggle, avatars |
| **Conversations inbox** | Unified inbox — DMs, comments, replies, emails. Channel filters, sentiment badges, star, search, reply |
| **Surveys (NPS, CSAT)** | NPS (0-10), CSAT (1-5), CES (1-7), custom — track responses & aggregate score |
| **Analytics** | Multi-range charts (traffic/conversions/revenue), funnel viz, channel mix, per-channel performance |

### ⚙️ Manage
| Section | What it does |
|---|---|
| **Tasks** | To-do tracker with priority, due date, project tagging |
| **Integrations** | 24 service connectors (Mailchimp, SendGrid, HubSpot, Meta, Google, Slack, etc.) with connect/disconnect flow |
| **Reports** | Auto-generated performance summaries, CSV export, jsPDF PDF export |
| **Settings** | Workspace, brand color, email defaults, API key vault (encrypted at rest) |

## Architecture

- **Next.js 14 App Router** with server components + client islands
- **SQLite** (`better-sqlite3`) for everything — single-file database at `data/hub.sqlite`
- **Tailwind CSS** + custom design system (no UI library), dynamic color safelist
- **Recharts** for all charts
- **lucide-react** for icons
- **framer-motion** for premium animations
- **jsPDF** for PDF reports
- **TypeScript** strict mode throughout
- RESTful API routes under `/api/*`
- Full CRUD on every resource
- Auto-migrations on first run (creates 35 tables)

## Database schema (35 tables)

`users, workspaces, settings, contacts, companies, deals, activities, campaigns, content, emails, landing_pages, seo_keywords, seo_audits, ads, assets, automations, integrations, api_keys, copy_templates, notes, tasks, brand_assets, personas, segments, forms, form_submissions, lead_magnets, funnels, experiments, testimonials, events, event_registrations, competitors, conversations, budget_items, playbooks, surveys, survey_responses`

## File map

```
src/
├── app/                              # 35 page routes
│   ├── page.tsx                      # Dashboard with module grid
```

- Dashboard, brand kit, personas, segments, forms, lead magnets, funnels, experiments, testimonials, events, competitors, inbox, budget, playbooks, surveys

metadata.output:
# Marketing Hub — All-in-One Marketing Command Center

> ⚡ **Core Doctrine: Intensity Beats Extensity — Every Time.**
> One channel mastered beats five channels touched. One offer sharpened beats ten offers scattered. One persona deeply understood beats five personas guessed. Read the full principle in [`docs/INTENSITY_PRINCIPLE.md`](docs/INTENSITY_PRINCIPLE.md).

A complete marketing platform built with **Next.js 14, TypeScript, Tailwind, and SQLite**. **30 interconnected sections** covering the entire marketing workflow — all running locally, no external API keys required.

## Quick start

```bash
cd ~/marketing-hub
npm install
npm run seed               # populate with 200+ sample records
npm run dev                # http://localhost:3000
```

For production:
```bash
npm run build
npm start
```

## What's inside (30 sections)

### 🧭 Plan
| Section | What it does |
|---|---|
| **Dashboard** | Unified metrics, module grid, traffic/conversion charts, active campaigns, upcoming content |
| **Brand kit** | Logos (primary/dark/icon), color palette, typography, voice & tone, usage guidelines |
| **Personas** | Audience profiles with goals, pain points, channels, messaging. Primary / secondary / anti-persona tiers |
| **Segments** | Rule-based audience segments (e.g. `score > 70 AND tag = newsletter`) |
| **Competitors** | Track competitors: positioning, pricing, SWOT analysis, strengths & weaknesses |
| **Campaigns** | Kanban (draft → scheduled → live → paused → completed), full CRUD, detail page, budget tracking |
| **Funnels** | Visual customer-journey builder — map awareness → landing → form → email → sale. Tracks conversion per step |
| **Budget planner** | Monthly budget by category, planned vs actual, variance tracking, category breakdown chart |
| **Playbooks** | Repeatable SOPs with steps, owners, durations, KPIs. Acquisition / activation / retention / revenue / branding |

### ✏️ Create
| Section | What it does |
|---|---|
| **Content calendar** | Monthly grid view with per-channel color coding, click-to-create |
| **Social studio** | Composer with per-channel character limits (Twitter 280, LinkedIn 3000, etc.), preview, scheduling |
| **Email builder** | Block-based editor (hero, features, CTA, testimonials, pricing, FAQ), live preview, HTML/text export |
| **Landing pages** | Same block editor for marketing pages, live preview, SEO meta, static HTML export |
| **Forms** | Drag-style form builder — text/email/phone/textarea/select/checkbox/number/URL fields, submit tracking |
| **Lead magnets** | Library of eBooks, templates, checklists, webinars, coupons, videos — track downloads |
| **Copy generator** | Offline template-based: headlines, hooks, CTAs, ad copy, social posts, email sequences, blog outlines |
| **Asset library** | File upload, image/video/document previews, search, tags |

### 📈 Grow
| Section | What it does |
|---|---|
| **SEO toolkit** | Keyword tracker (volume/difficulty/opportunity), HTML auditor, sitemap.xml, robots.txt |
| **Ads manager** | Multi-channel (Meta/Google/TikTok/LinkedIn), spend/impressions/clicks/CTR/CPA, pause/resume |
| **Events & webinars** | Webinars, conferences, meetups, workshops, launches — registration tracking, capacity fill rate |
| **CRM & deals** | Contacts with scoring, companies, deals kanban (drag between stages), activity tracking |
| **Automations** | Trigger→action rule builder (8 triggers × 8 actions), run tracking, pause/resume |
| **A/B testing** | Experiments with two variants, traffic & conversions, auto-computed lift & confidence, winner flag |
| **Testimonials** | Social proof library with ratings, sources (G2/Trustpilot/Twitter/email/manual), featured toggle, avatars |
| **Conversations inbox** | Unified inbox — DMs, comments, replies, emails. Channel filters, sentiment badges, star, search, reply |
| **Surveys (NPS, CSAT)** | NPS (0-10), CSAT (1-5), CES (1-7), custom — track responses & aggregate score |
| **Analytics** | Multi-range charts (traffic/conversions/revenue), funnel viz, channel mix, per-channel performance |

### ⚙️ Manage
| Section | What it does |
|---|---|
| **Tasks** | To-do tracker with priority, due date, project tagging |
| **Integrations** | 24 service connectors (Mailchimp, SendGrid, HubSpot, Meta, Google, Slack, etc.) with connect/disconnect flow |
| **Reports** | Auto-generated performance summaries, CSV export, jsPDF PDF export |
| **Settings** | Workspace, brand color, email defaults, API key vault (encrypted at rest) |

## Architecture

- **Next.js 14 App Router** with server components + client islands
- **SQLite** (`better-sqlite3`) for everything — single-file database at `data/hub.sqlite`
- **Tailwind CSS** + custom design system (no UI library), dynamic color safelist
- **Recharts** for all charts
- **lucide-react** for icons
- **framer-motion** for premium animations
- **jsPDF** for PDF reports
- **TypeScript** strict mode throughout
- RESTful API routes under `/api/*`
- Full CRUD on every resource
- Auto-migrations on first run (creates 35 tables)

## Database schema (35 tables)

`users, workspaces, settings, contacts, companies, deals, activities, campaigns, content, emails, landing_pages, seo_keywords, seo_audits, ads, assets, automations, integrations, api_keys, copy_templates, notes, tasks, brand_assets, personas, segments, forms, form_submissions, lead_magnets, funnels, experiments, testimonials, events, event_registrations, competitors, conversations, budget_items, playbooks, surveys, survey_responses`

## File map

```
src/
├── app/                              # 35 page routes
│   ├── page.tsx                      # Dashboard with module grid
│   ├── brand/                        # Brand kit (logos, colors, type, voice)
│   ├── personas/                     # Audience personas
│   ├── segments/                     # Rule-based segments
│   ├── forms/                        # Form builder
│   ├── lead-magnets/                 # eBooks, templates, etc.
│   ├── funnels/                      # Visual customer journeys
│   ├── experiments/                  # A/B testing
│   ├── testimonials/                 # Social proof
│   ├── events/                       # Webinars & conferences
│   ├── competitors/                  # Competitive intelligence + SWOT
│   ├── inbox/                        # Unified conversations
│   ├── budget/                       # Spend planner
│   ├── playbooks/                    # SOPs
│   ├── surveys/                      # NPS, CSAT, CES
│   ├── campaigns/[id]/page.tsx       # Campaign detail
│   ├── emails/[id]/page.tsx          # Email builder
│   ├── landing-pages/[id]/page.tsx   # Landing page builder
│   ├── preview/[id]/page.tsx         # Iframe preview
│   └── api/                          # 71+ API routes (full CRUD on every resource)
├── components/
│   ├── Sidebar.tsx                   # 30 modules in 4 groups
│   ├── Topbar.tsx, Modal.tsx, Stat.tsx, Badge.tsx
│   ├── BlockEditor.tsx               # Block-based content editor
│   ├── CommandPalette.tsx            # ⌘K with all 30 sections
│   ├── useFetch.ts, Anim.tsx, Skeleton.tsx, Toast.tsx, ThemeProvider.tsx
└── lib/
    ├── db/index.ts                   # SQLite + 35-table schema + auto-migrations
    ├── api-helpers.ts                # listRows, insertRow, updateRow, deleteRow
    ├── constants.ts                  # Channels, deal stages, integrations, formatters
    ├── blocks.ts                     # Block model + HTML renderers
    ├── copy.ts                       # Copy generation (headlines, hooks, CTAs, etc.)
    ├── seo.ts                        # SEO audit, sitemap, robots
    └── analytics.ts                  # Deterministic mock data for charts

scripts/seed.ts                       # Seeds 250+ records across all 30 sections
data/hub.sqlite                       # SQLite database (auto-created)
```

## Verified working

All 35 pages and 71+ APIs return 200. Tested:
- Dashboard, brand kit, personas, segments, forms, lead magnets, funnels, experiments, testimonials, events, competitors, inbox, budget, playbooks, surveys
- All existing: campaigns, calendar, studio, emails, landing pages, copy, SEO, ads, CRM, analytics, automations, assets, integrations, reports, settings, tasks
- Detail pages (campaigns/[id], emails/[id], landing-pages/[id], preview/[id])
- Create / Read / Update / Delete on every new resource
- Form submissions increment leads_count
- Survey responses auto-recompute aggregate score
- A/B test lift + confidence auto-computed
- ⌘K palette shows all 30 sections + searches across all records
- Module grid on dashboard with live counts from each section

## Coverage matrix

| Marketing area | Hub coverage |
|---|---|
| Strategy & planning | Brand kit, Personas, Competitors, Playbooks, Funnels |
| Audience & segmentation | Personas, Segments, CRM |
| Content creation | Calendar, Studio, Email builder, Landing pages, Forms, Copy generator |
| Distribution & channels | Social, Email, Landing pages, Forms, Events, Inbox |
| Lead capture | Forms, Lead magnets, Landing pages |
| Conversion & sales | Funnels, Experiments, A/B tests |
| Paid acquisition | Ads manager, Budget |
| Earned & owned | SEO, Content, Email, Events |
| Customer feedback | Surveys, Testimonials, Inbox |
| Operations & measurement | Analytics, Reports, Budget, Tasks, Integrations |

→ **30 sections, 35 DB tables, 71 API routes, 250+ seeded records.**

state.title: Read public package.json

reasoning_details.text:
Interesting. marketing-hub-public lacks agency and cockpit, etc. But the user specifically asked about shipping the marketing hub and agency feature. I need to plan carefully.

I should inspect more of the marketing-hub app structure to understand all existing components. Let's read some relevant pages, lib files, and maybe see how big some directories are. I might want to use `ls` repeatedly to map out the full set of app routes in both repos.

Also, the user request is very broad: "all the tools needed for the professional marketer." I need to compare against a standard professional marketing stack.

But I am in planning mode. I should first gather context. Then ask one focused question if needed.

Key decisions unresolved:
- Is "shipping" meaning deploying marketing-hub-public and marketing-hub together? Are they separate apps?
- Should agency be a separate product/themed front-end, or just a page/route within the hub?
- What does "all the tools needed for the professional marketer" include beyond existing sections? AI features? PR? Client reporting? Project management? Billing?
- Should we wrap this in an agency/managed-services layer (clients, white-label, reporting) or just ensure the hub itself is fully featured?
- Are there database/auth differences between marketing-hub and marketing-hub-public?
- Should we use Turbo/ monorepo, or keep them separate?

Let's inspect package.json of public, .env files, AGENTS.md, etc.

Marketing Hub — All-in-One Marketing Command Center. A Next.js + React application.

- Next.js + React (28 dependencies)

I need to understand what the user means by "all the tools needed for the professional marketer." The marketing-hub README says it's already an all-in-one marketing command center with 30 sections. But the user still wants to ship the marketing hub and agency feature. Maybe they haven't actually shipped/productionized yet, or maybe they want it marketed/positioned as a complete professional suite.

Since I'm in planning mode, I should first determine the current state and then ask a focused question.

Key facts:
- marketing-hub is a Next.js app with many sections, but sits at `/home/uzer/marketing-hub`.
- marketing-hub-public is a similar Next.js app, likely the branded/public version (maybe built for deployment/public showcase), but missing many sections (only ~16 app directories).
- Agency page exists in marketing-hub as a marketing landing page, but not in marketing-hub-public.
- User wants to "ship" marketing hub and agency feature with all professional marketer tools.

Possible interpretations:
1. They want to ship (deploy) the marketing-hub app publicly, including the agency section/page, and ensure it contains every tool a professional marketer needs.
2. They want to build out the agency feature as a full product module within the hub (e.g., client management, proposals, white-label reporting).
3. They want to consolidate marketing-hub into marketing-hub-public (or vice versa) so the public site IS the marketing hub.

I need to ask exactly one question to clarify. What is the most important unresolved decision?

The most important: Is the goal to unify these two folders into one shippable app, or are they capturing different things (private workspace vs public agency site)? Also, what does "agency feature" mean — a page, an internal CRM for agency clients, or both?

I should ask: "When you say ship the marketing hub and agency feature, do you want a single deployable app, or keep the internal workspace (marketing-hub) and public-facing site (marketing-hub-public) separate? And does 'agency' refer to a public landing page, or an internal agency operations module (clients, proposals, white-label reporting)?"

But I must ask one question at a time. The question should include my recommended answer.

Recommended answer: Merge public into hub or hub into public into a single Next.js app, and add an internal agency ops module (clients, proposals, reports). Or maybe just stable deployment.

Title: Top Rated Tools for Marketing Agencies 2026 — What 500+ Agencies Actually Use | Kobin
URL: https://www.kobin.team/blog/best-tools-for-marketing-agencies
Published: 2026-03-10T06:56:53.000Z
Author: Arham Mirkar
Highlights:
The top rated tools for marketing agencies in 2026 are Kobin AI (9.4/10, all-in-one workspace), Semrush (9.1/10, SEO intelligence), Google Workspace (8.9/10, foundation), Canva Teams (8.6/10, design), and HubSpot (8.4/10, CRM for 15+ person agencies). Most marketing agencies need 4–5 tools: one workspace OS, one SEO tool, one design tool, and one social media tool.
...
Marketing agencies run some of the most tool-dense operations of any business type. A typical 10-person marketing agency runs Slack for communication, Notion for briefs and knowledge, Asana for project delivery, HubSpot for CRM, Buffer for social scheduling, Canva for design, Semrush for SEO intelligence, and Google Drive for file sharing. That is eight separate platforms, eight separate logins, and eight separate AI systems that can only see their own data.
...
This guide ranks the tools that actually move the needle for marketing agency productivity — not by feature count, but by a composite rating across the five factors that determine whether a tool actually improves your agency operations or just adds another tab to manage.
...
We rated each tool across five dimensions equally weighted at 20% each:Client Delivery Quality (how well does it support delivering finished work to clients — portals, approvals, file management, status visibility?); Team Collaboration (real-time communication, task assignment, workload visibility, information sharing?); AI Capability (how smart is the AI, how much of the workspace does it see, and can it take action or only answer?); Value for Money (total monthly cost for a 10-person marketing agency including all add-ons needed to make it functional?); and Setup Time (how long before the tool actually helps rather than requiring configuration and training?). Each dimension was scored 1–10. The composite score is the unweighted average.
...
## #1 Kobin AI — 9.4/10
...
Highest overall score because it eliminates the coordination cost between tools — every marketing team member, client, task, brief, and campaign lives in one workspace with one AI that can act on all of it.
...
## #2 Semrush — 9.1/10
...
Irreplaceable for SEO-focused marketing agencies. The best competitive intelligence, keyword research, and site audit platform available. Use alongside Kobin AI — Semrush is a vertical tool, not a workspace.
...
## #3 Google Workspace — 8.9/10
...
Non-negotiable foundation. Every marketing agency uses Gmail, Google Calendar, and Google Meet regardless of what else they use. The key is not whether to use Google Workspace, but what to build on top of it.
...
## #4 HubSpot — 8.4/10
...
Gold standard CRM for mid-to-large marketing agencies with dedicated sales teams and 50+ leads/month in active pipeline. Overkill and expensive for agencies under 15 people. The built-in CRM in Kobin AI covers 90% of the use case at zero extra cost.
...
## #5 Canva Teams — 8.6/10
...
Essential for marketing agencies with content and social production workflows. Canva Teams brand kit, template library, and collaborative editing are best-in-class for non-designer-heavy teams. Combines well with Kobin AI Vault for asset delivery.
...
## #6 Productive.io — 8.2/10
...
Marketing agencies have predictable operational workflows that repeat across every client and every campaign.The most time-intensive are: campaign brief management (brief creation to team briefing), client reporting and deliverable approval (file delivery to signed-off approval), lead and prospect management (inbound lead to booked call), team standups and workload visibility (daily alignment without ceremony), social media content management (draft to published with client approval), and new client onboarding (deal signed to project live). Most marketing agencies use 3–6 different tools to cover these six workflows — and the coordination cost between those tools is where 2–4 hours per person per week disappears. The agencies that run the most efficiently in 2026 are those that have reduced their workflow-tool count to 3 or fewer.
...
Campaign Planning & Brief ManagementNotion (brief) + Asana (tasks) + Slack (discussion)One project workspace: brief in Vault, tasks in project board, discussion in project inbox roomEliminates 3-tab switching for every campaign kickoff
...
Client Reporting & Deliverable ApprovalGoogle Drive (files) + Email (approval) + Loom (walkthrough)Vault Deliverables folder → client portal shows files → built-in approval workflow with timestampApproval audit trail + no broken Drive links + no email chain
...
Lead & Prospect ManagementHubSpot ($50–135/mo) + Gmail (manual)Kobin CRM + Gmail sync + auto-lead detection from incoming emails$50–135/month saved + zero manual entry for inbound leads
...
Team Standup & Workload VisibilitySlack (standup bot) + Asana (tasks)Kobin AI morning brief at 8am + team workload chart in task analyticsNo standup ceremony — AI surfaces priorities and blockers automatically
...
LinkedIn Content (client social management)Buffer/Taplio ($18–$50/mo) + Notion (content calendar)Kobin LinkedIn Studio: draft, schedule, publish, track impressions$18–50/month + content calendar lives next to project tasks
...
New Client OnboardingEmail (access links) + Notion (onboarding doc) + Asana (task creation)One-click client portal activation with pre-created DM, tasks, and vault structureOnboarding goes from 45 minutes of setup to 90 seconds
...
## The recommended marketing agency tech stack for 2026
...
Based on the ratings above and the workflow analysis, here is the optimal tech stack for marketing agencies at each size tier in 2026:
...
The top rated productivity tools for marketing agencies in 2026 are: Kobin AI (9.4/10 — all-in-one AI workspace replacing Slack, Notion, Asana, HubSpot, and Buffer from $29/month), Semrush (9.1/10 — irreplaceable for SEO agencies), Google Workspace (8.9/10 — non-negotiable foundation), Canva Teams (8.6/10 — best design collaboration for non-designers), and HubSpot (8.4/10 — best CRM for mid-market agencies with dedicated sales teams). The recommended tech stack for a 10-person marketing agency is Kobin AI + Google Workspace + Semrush + Canva, totaling approximately $380/month.
...
Most marketing agencies in 2026 use Google Workspace (email, calendar, Meet), Slack (messaging), Notion (documentation), Asana or ClickUp (project management), HubSpot (CRM), Buffer or Hootsuite (social scheduling), Semrush (SEO), and Canva (design). This stack costs $400–600/month for a 10-person agency and requires constant context switching between platforms. The trend in 2026 is consolidation — agencies replacing Slack + Notion + Asana + HubSpot + Buffer with a unified workspace like Kobin AI.
...
For marketing agencies, the best project management approach in 2026 is a unified workspace that connects project management to client communication and delivery — rather than a standalone PM tool. Asana is the best standalone project management tool for marketing agencies (strong workflow automation, clean timeline view). However, most agencies need Asana + Slack + Google Drive + HubSpot alongside it to cover all agency workflows, which totals $500+/month. Kobin AI covers project management as part of its unified workspace from $29/month, including client portal, CRM, and inbox.
...
How do I build an efficient marketing agency tech stack?

An efficient marketing agency tech stack has 3–5 tools maximum, with no overlapping functionality and no coordination tools between them. The optimal structure: one workspace OS (Kobin AI — handles communication, tasks, CRM, client delivery, and AI), one SEO intelligence tool (Semrush or Ahrefs, depending on focus), one design tool (Canva Teams for content, Figma for product/UI), and Google Workspace as the email/calendar foundation. Avoid building a stack where you need Zapier or middleware to connect tools — every integration point is a failure point and a time cost.

Vendors blur the lines between project management, time tracking, billing, and financial planning so every demo feels like the answer. It isn't. Picking the wrong tool is how a 12-person creative shop ends up paying $32,000 a year for software that doesn't tell them which clients are losing them money. This guide breaks advertising agency software into the four jobs it actually does, when each one matters, and what to buy in which order — written for owners and operators of $1M–$50M agencies, not for IT buyers at WPP.
...
Every tool in the category claims to do "agency management." In practice, agency software does exactly four things, and each tool is genuinely strong at one or two of them:
...
1. Run the work — projects, tasks, briefs, approvals, retainers. (Job runners: Asana, Monday, ClickUp, Notion, Wrike, Teamwork, Workamajig, Function Point)
2. Track the time and the money in — hours, billable rates, retainers consumed, invoices, AR. (Time + billing: Harvest, Toggl, Productive, Scoro, Accelo, BigTime)
3. Track the money out and the margins — staff costs, contractor costs, vendor pass-through, true project profitability. (Profitability: Productive, Scoro, Forecast, Float, Workamajig, Parakeeto)
...
4. Plan the business — capacity, forecast, cash, scenario planning. (FP&A: spreadsheets for 95% of agencies, then CentSight or a fractional CFO stack)
...
- 0–10 people: Run the work in whatever your team already opens daily (most likely ClickUp, Asana, or Notion). Track time in Harvest or Toggl. Bill from QuickBooks or Xero. Skip dedicated agency software. The setup cost is bigger than the upside.
...
- 10–30 people: You need job #3 (profitability). The tool you pick here is the most important software decision your
...
makes for the next three years. Top of the realistic shortlist: Productive, Scoro, Parakeeto (a
...
layer on top
...
your existing stack rather
...
), or a
...
person owns.
...
- 30+ people: Now the all-in-one suites earn their keep — Workamajig, Scoro Ultimate, Productive Enterprise. You also need job #4 (FP&A and cash planning) running outside the project tool. That's where an AI CFO or fractional CFO fits.
...
This is the category most owners don't fully understand before buying. Profitability software is not invoicing software. It does four things:
...
- Calculates a true cost per hour for every person — salary + payroll tax + benefits + overhead allocation, divided by productive hours (not 2,080).
- Multiplies that cost by the hours each person logged to a project to get true project cost.
- Subtracts true project cost from project revenue (recognized, not billed) to get gross profit by project.
- Rolls up by client, by service line, by team, and by month.
...
If your "agency software" doesn't do all four, it's a project management tool with invoicing. Useful — but not what stops you from losing money on your top-three retainer client.
...
- ClickUp — best per-user value, customizable to the point of overwhelming, strong native time tracking. The default pick for most $1M–$5M agencies in 2026.
- Asana — cleanest UX, weakest at agency-specific features (rate cards, billable utilization), strong if your work is campaign-shaped rather than retainer-shaped.
- Monday — the marketing-led winner. Most agencies who pick Monday picked it because their CEO saw a demo, not because it's the best fit. Re-evaluate at 20 people.
...
- Notion — fine as a docs + light tasks combo. Not a project tool. Stop pretending.
...
- Teamwork — purpose-built for client work, dying brand recognition, but the
...
product is well-aimed at this exact category.
...
- Workamajig / Function Point —
...
Time tracking is the most undervalued part of the advertising agency software stack. Without clean hours data, profitability calculations are guesses. With it, every other tool in the stack works.
...
- Hours logged within 48 hours of the work happening (not at month-end).
- Logged against a project and a service-line tag (strategy, creative, production, paid media, account management).
- Adoption above 85% across all billable staff.
...
Tools that hit this regularly: Harvest, Toggl Track, Hubstaff (with caveats — passive tracking is culturally explosive in creative shops; opt for active mode). Tools that nominally have time tracking but fail on adoption: most all-in-one suites, because the time entry is buried under three menus.
...
For a 10-person agency, run Harvest standalone and connect it to QuickBooks. Skip the suite until you've got six months of clean hours data. Buying agency software before you have clean hours data is buying a Ferrari for a road you haven't paved.
...
The thing every advertising agency software demo glosses over is job #4 — actual FP&A. Cash forecasting six months out by client. Capacity planning for the senior creative team across Q3. Scenario modeling on what happens if your second-largest retainer doesn't renew. The model that tells you whether to hire two more strategists in September.
...
- A part-time bookkeeper plus a spreadsheet that the founder maintains personally (most common, brittle, doesn't scale).
- A fractional CFO at $3k–$8k/month who builds and maintains the model (works, expensive, gated on availability).
- A connected AI CFO layer that pulls from QuickBooks and the project tool and produces the forecast continuously (where CentSight sits —
...
Q: How is advertising agency software different from generic project management? A: Three things. Rate cards (billable rate per role, per client). Retainer tracking (hours consumed vs. retainer purchased, monthly). Profitability by client/project (true cost vs. recognized revenue). If a tool doesn't do all three, it's project management with an agency skin.
...
Q: Where does AI fit in agency software in 2026? A: Two real places. (1) Automating time entry from calendar + Slack + tool activity (Productive and Harvest both have versions). (2) AI for FP&A and cash forecasting on top of the agency stack. Most "AI features" in agency PM tools right now are demo-driven and not yet load-bearing.
...
Q: What about HoneyBook, Dubsado, 17hats? A: Aimed at solopreneurs and 1–5-person studios — proposals, contracts, intake forms, basic invoicing. If you're past 10 people, you've outgrown them.
...
Stop shopping advertising agency software as one decision. Buy job #1 (run the work) cheaply and quickly so the team adopts it. Buy job #2 (time and billing) right behind it, with adoption as the only KPI. Earn the right to buy job #3 (profitability) by getting six months of clean hours data first. Solve job #4 (financial planning) with a fractional CFO or an AI CFO layer outside the project tool — because none of the project tools genuinely do it well, no matter what the demo shows.

Title: Bonsai Review 2026: Pricing, Features & Alternatives
URL: https://firmtools.ai/bonsai/
Published: 2026-05-14T20:21:13.000Z
Author: Jordan Reeves
Highlights:
Quick verdict: Bonsai is an all-in-one client management platform for freelancers, consultants, and small agencies, covering proposals, contracts, invoicing, time tracking, CRM, and basic accounting in a single subscription. It is strongest for solo operators and two-to-four-person teams that want a polished, unified workflow without stitching together separate apps. The two big caveats: Bonsai was acquired by Zoom in December 2025 and its long-term roadmap is now controlled by an enterprise communications company, and the per-user pricing structure escalates quickly past three or four seats. The $15 Basic plan is also misleadingly marketed since it does not include invoicing.
...
Bonsai unifies proposals, contracts, invoicing, and time tracking for freelancers and small agencies.
...
Bonsai is a business management platform built specifically for service professionals who run their own books. It bundles the tools a typical freelancer or boutique agency would otherwise pay for separately: a CRM and pipeline, proposal and contract templates with e-signature, a client portal, time tracking and timesheets, recurring invoicing, expense tracking, and lightweight project management. There is also a US-only tax module available as a paid add-on and a Bonsai-branded prepaid card for business spending.
...
The pitch Bonsai makes is simple: stop running your business across six tools when one will do. A proposal becomes a contract, signed hours become invoiced hours, paid invoices land in the books, and the client sees a single branded portal throughout. For service businesses where admin time is the silent tax on every engagement, that integration is the core value proposition.
...
The pricing structure has a quirk that is easy to miss. The Basic plan at $15/user does not include invoicing. It is a CRM, time-tracker, and basic project tool only. For most freelancers, the realistic entry point is Essentials at $25/user/month, because that is where proposals, contracts, invoicing, scheduling, and the client portal actually unlock. The “$15 plan” marketing exists, but it does not represent what most buyers are actually shopping for.
...
Most “all-in-one” platforms are really three tools sharing a login. Bonsai’s integration runs deeper. Accepted proposals auto-generate contracts. Signed contracts unlock client portal access. Logged time rolls directly into invoices. Recurring invoices trigger automated payment reminders. For solo operators without an admin assistant, that continuity is the single biggest time saver, and it is the feature most consistently praised in user reviews.
...
Bonsai ships with a sizable template library covering proposals, contracts, NDAs, statements of work, scopes of work, and project briefs. Proposals support multiple pricing formats including tiered options and a la carte selections, which is useful for service businesses that want clients to self-select packages. The templates are also fully editable and reusable, so the time investment in customizing one proposal pays off on every subsequent client. E-signature is built in and works without needing a separate tool like DocuSign or HelloSign.
...
The branded client portal is one of Bonsai’s clearest selling points for solo freelancers. Clients log into a single space to see their proposals, contracts, invoices, project status, and message history. Done well, this makes a one-person operation look like an agency, which directly affects rates and retention. White-label branding (removing Bonsai’s logo and using your own domain) requires Premium or higher, which is a real upsell pressure for anyone who cares about presentation.
...
Bonsai’s interface is one of the cleaner ones in this category. Most users can have their first proposal sent and first invoice issued within an hour of signing up. The dashboard prioritizes the active work (open proposals, unpaid invoices, current projects) over feature inventory, which keeps new users from drowning in options. For freelancers who churn through SaaS trying to find something that does not feel like enterprise software, this matters.

The QuickBooks integration syncs invoices, bills, payments, customers, products, taxes, classes, and chart of accounts bidirectionally -- but only with QuickBooks Online, not QuickBooks Desktop. The Xero integration syncs the same data plus tracking categories and supports multi-entity setups (one Scoro entity per Xero organization). Both can be set as manual or automatic. Source: Scoro QuickBooks integration, Scoro Xero integration.
...
Real-world implementation runs 4-8 weeks for a small team self-onboarding and 8-16 weeks for a mid-sized agency using Scoro's paid implementation services. The configuration touches CRM fields, project templates, billing rules, time tracking categories, dashboards, and accounting integration -- and gets harder if your team has historical data to import. Plan for at least one full quarter before declaring success.
...
It works for time entry, basic task views, and contact lookups, but it lacks the planner, retainer management, automation building, and most reporting. Almost every long-term Scoro user defaults back to the web app for anything beyond logging hours. If your team is mobile-first or field-first, this is a meaningful limitation.
...
Most agencies who buy Scoro for the reasons Scoro is famous -- profitability tracking, retainer management, capacity planning, sales pipeline -- need Pro, not Essential or Standard. Essential and Standard are skeleton plans missing the financial features that justify Scoro's premium positioning. Plan to evaluate Scoro at the Pro price point ($63/user/month, $315/month minimum) when running ROI math.
...
The 5-seat minimum, the 4-
...
week learning curve, and the mobile-app weakness are also not noise. They reflect a real positioning decision: Scoro does not want to be the tool for solo consultants, 2-person studios, or field-heavy operations. The product is built for office-based, billable-hour, retainer-driven service businesses with enough scale to absorb the onboarding cost.
...

data.text:
I need to understand the current state better. The user wants me to:
1. Scrape the net for features they don't have
2. Add them
3. Make them work
4. Make this the best marketing agency ever

But I'm in Plan Mode, so I need to gather context and produce an implementation-ready plan.

From my research so far, I've identified that the current marketing hub has:
- Marketing dashboard, brand kit, personas, segments, campaigns, funnels, budget, content calendar, social studio, email builder, landing pages, forms, lead magnets, copy generator, SEO, ads, CRM, analytics, automations, A/B testing, testimonials, events, competitors, inbox, surveys, tasks, integrations, reports, settings, etc.
- Some agency features: agency page (public), studio pro, marketplace, orchestrator, billing, timesheets, client invoices, project PnL, approvals, portal links, agency client accounts

But it's missing many critical agency features compared to top platforms:
1. **Client Portal** (fully white-label, branded, secure)
2. **Proposals & Contracts** (with e-signature)
3. **Project Management** (proper PM with timelines, milestones, dependencies)
4. **Time Tracking** (UI for logging time against projects/clients)
5. **Retainer Management** (tracking hours vs retainer purchased)
6. **Client Onboarding** (automated workflows)
7. **Client Reporting** (automated branded PDF reports)
8. **White-label branding** (custom domain, logo, colors)
9. **Profitability Analytics** (true margin per client/project)
10. **Resource/Capacity Planning**
11. **Client Messaging/Communication** (within portal)
12. **File Sharing/Approvals** (client approval workflows)
13. **Proposal templates** (with line items, fees)
14. **Contract templates** (e-signature)
15. **Employee/Contractor Management**
16. **Public Booking/Intake Pages**
17. **Multi-brand Workspaces**
18. **Advanced Financials** (expenses, P&L, AR aging)

Wait, some of these exist partially. Let me look at what API routes exist.

state.title: List API routes start

reasoning_details.text:
I need to understand the current state better. The user wants me to:
1. Scrape the net for features they don't have
2. Add them
3. Make them work
4. Make this the best marketing agency ever

But I'm in Plan Mode, so I need to gather context and produce an implementation-ready plan.

From my research so far, I've identified that the current marketing hub has:
- Marketing dashboard, brand kit, personas, segments, campaigns, funnels, budget, content calendar, social studio, email builder, landing pages, forms, lead magnets, copy generator, SEO, ads, CRM, analytics, automations, A/B testing, testimonials, events, competitors, inbox, surveys, tasks, integrations, reports, settings, etc.
- Some agency features: agency page (public), studio pro, marketplace, orchestrator, billing, timesheets, client invoices, project PnL, approvals, portal links, agency client accounts

But it's missing many critical agency features compared to top platforms:
1. **Client Portal** (fully white-label, branded, secure)
2. **Proposals & Contracts** (with e-signature)
3. **Project Management** (proper PM with timelines, milestones, dependencies)
4. **Time Tracking** (UI for logging time against projects/clients)
5. **Retainer Management** (tracking hours vs retainer purchased)
6. **Client Onboarding** (automated workflows)
7. **Client Reporting** (automated branded PDF reports)
8. **White-label branding** (custom domain, logo, colors)
9. **Profitability Analytics** (true margin per client/project)
10. **Resource/Capacity Planning**
11. **Client Messaging/Communication** (within portal)
12. **File Sharing/Approvals** (client approval workflows)
13. **Proposal templates** (with line items, fees)
14. **Contract templates** (e-signature)
15. **Employee/Contractor Management**
16. **Public Booking/Intake Pages**
17. **Multi-brand Workspaces**
18. **Advanced Financials** (expenses, P&L, AR aging)

Wait, some of these exist partially. Let me look at what API routes exist.

Good, so finance pages exist and seem to have working APIs. Let me check what APIs exist for finance and what pages might be missing. I also need to understand the "agency feature shipping" - looking at the sidebar, there's a `/roi-marketing` page and an `/agency` page. 

The user said "the marketing hub and agency feature shipping i need it to have all the tools needed for the professional marketer". This seems to be about making the marketing hub + agency functionality complete.

Based on my research, the biggest missing pieces from top agency platforms are:
1. **proper client portal** (not just portal links - but a full branded portal experience)
2. **proposals & contracts with e-signature**
3. **proper project management** (not just tasks)
4. **client onboarding workflows**
5. **client messaging/communication hub**
6. **white-label branding** (custom domain, colors, logo)
7. **public booking/intake pages**
8. **proposal templates**
9. **contract templates**
10. **client reporting/automated reports**
11. **resource/capacity planning**
12. **employee management** (the /finance/employees exists but let me verify)
13. **SMS/WhatsApp** (/sms exists in sidebar but let me verify)

data.text:
The pages exist. Now let me understand the full picture better. I need to check what working APIs exist for the finance and other sections, and what pages might be missing.

Actually, given the scope of this task, I think I need to ask the user a clarifying question before creating the plan. The user said "scrape the net for features i dont have add them make them work till this app becomes the best marketing agency ever". 

This is an incredibly broad request. Based on my research, the two biggest gaps are:
1. **Client Portal** (proper branded, white-label experience)
2. **Proposals & Contracts** (with e-signature)
3. **Project Management** (proper PM beyond tasks)
4. **Client Onboarding**
5. **Client Communication/Messaging**
6. **White-label branding**
7. **Public booking/intake**
8. **Automated client reporting**

But I should clarify with the user what they mean by "agency feature" - is it:
- Internal agency operations (time tracking, invoicing, profitability)?
- Client-facing portal?
- Both?

Wait, I'm in Plan Mode and the instructions say: "Interview the user about every important aspect of the plan until you reach shared understanding... Ask one question at a time, and include your recommended answer."

So I should ask ONE question to clarify the most important ambiguity.

The critical question is: When you say "agency feature", do you mean:
1. Internal agency operations (time tracking, invoicing, profitability, contractor management) - which ALREADY has some pages/DB tables
2. Client-facing experience (white-label portal, proposals, contracts, client approvals, public booking)
3. Both

But actually, looking at the code, the internal finance pages DO exist and seem functional. The biggest gap in the market research was the client-facing portal, proposals, contracts, and white-label branding.

Wait - the user said "scrape the net for features i dont have add them make them work". So they want me to:
1. Figure out what's missing
2. Add it
3. Make it work

I have enough context from the web research to identify the gaps. Let me check one more thing - the public-facing `marketing-hub-public` app since that's likely the "agency" front-end.

Actually, I should also check what the `agency` page in the sidebar `/roi-marketing` points to and if there's actually a working client portal.

Let me check the portal links API and public routes.

data.text:
The grep results were blocked. Let me just use cat with head to look at specific parts of the db schema. Actually, I have enough context now. Let me write the plan.

Based on my extensive research and codebase inspection:

**EXISTING:**
- Database: 90+ tables, many agency-related (agency_client_accounts, timesheets, client_invoices, project_pnl, approvals, portal_links, contractors, payouts, billing_subscriptions, billing_invoices, etc.)
- Finance pages: /finance/timesheets, /finance/invoices, /finance/profitability, /finance/contractors, /finance/payouts
- Agency page: /agency (public landing page)
- ROI Marketing: /roi-marketing
- Billing: /billing
- Campaigns, CRM, Contacts, Companies, Deals, Tasks, Automations, Inbox, Surveys, etc. - all exist
- Some API routes exist for the finance tables

**MISSING (compared to top agency platforms like Kobin AI, AgencyPro, SuiteDash, Scoro, Assembly):**

**Category 1: Client Experience Layer**
1. **White-label Client Portal** - Currently only basic `/c/<token>` with raw scope strings. Needs: custom domain config, branded UI, dashboard, progress, files, communication
2. **Proposals & E-Signature** - Completely missing. Need proposal builder, templates, e-signature, tracking
3. **Contracts** - Missing. Need contract templates, e-signature, linked to proposals/clients
4. **Client Onboarding** - Missing. Need workflows, checklists, auto-enrollment
5. **Client Messaging** - Missing. Need in-portal messaging between agency and client
6. **Client Reporting** - Missing. Need automated branded PDF reports, scheduled delivery
7. **Public Booking/Intake** - Missing or very basic. Need booking pages, intake forms
8. **Client File Sharing** - Missing. Need per-client file vault with access control

**Category 2: Agency Operations Layer**
9. **Project Management** - Missing beyond basic tasks. Need: projects linked to clients, milestones, timelines, Kanban/Gantt
10. **Retainer Management** - Missing. Need: retainer tracking, hours consumed vs purchased, MRR, overage billing
11. **Rate Cards** - Missing. Need: billable rates per role/service line, rate card management
12. **Capacity Planning** - Missing. Need: resource allocation, team workload, capacity forecasting
13. **Expense Management** - Missing UI. Table exists? Let me check.
14. **Client Approvals** - Missing. Need: proof delivery, structured feedback, revision tracking
15. **Service Catalog** - Missing. Need: list of services offered, pricing tiers, packages

**Category 3: AI & Automation Enhancements**
16. **AI Proposal Generator** - Missing
17. **AI Client Onboarding Assistant** - Missing
18. **AI Report Narratives** - Missing

But the user asked for ONE plan. Let me create a focused, actionable plan that addresses the biggest gaps. I'll prioritize the features that make the most impact for a "best marketing agency ever" platform:

**Tier 1 (Must-have for agency operations):**
- Proposals & E-Signature
- Contracts
- Client Portal (enhanced)
- Project Management (proper)
- Client Messaging
- White-Label Settings

**Tier 2 (High value):**
- Retainer Management
- Rate Cards
- Client Onboarding
- Client Reporting
- Capacity Planning

**Tier 3 (Nice to have):**
- Public Booking/Intake
- Client Approvals
- Service Catalog
- AI proposal generator

input.content:
# Marketing Hub — Agency Completeness Plan

**Goal:** Ship every feature a professional marketing agency needs to run client work, bill accurately, and deliver a premium client experience — all inside this Next.js + SQLite hub.

**Context:**
- `marketing-hub` (private workspace) already has 90+ DB tables, 120+ API routes, 44+ pages.
- Core marketing execution is solid: SEO, ads, CRM, campaigns, email, landing pages, funnels, analytics, automations, A/B tests, inbox, surveys, reports, copy generator.
- Agency/finance layer is partially built: **timesheets**, **client invoices**, **project PnL**, **contractors**, **payouts**, **billing**, **portal links**, **agency client accounts**, **approvals**.
- What’s missing is the **client-facing experience layer** and **agency operations depth** that top platforms (Kobin AI, AgencyPro, SuiteDash, Assembly, Scoro) already ship.

---

## Phase 1: Client Experience Layer (The “Agency” in Marketing Agency)

### 1.1 White-Label Client Portal
**Status:** `portal_links` table + `/api/portal-links` exists, but `/c/[token]` is a minimal read-only shell.
**Add:**
- Dedicated portal pages: `/portal/[token]` with full dashboard showing project status, invoices, files, upcoming events, and messages.
- Workspace-level white-label settings in Settings: custom domain, logo, primary color, email sender name.
- Secure, token-scoped data access — clients see only their own data.
- “Request support” / message thread inside portal.

### 1.2 Proposals + E-Signature
**Status:** Completely missing.
**Add:**
- DB table: `proposals` (id, workspace, client_id, title, line_items JSON, total, currency, status, due_date, terms, cover_letter, template_id, created_at).
- DB table: `proposal_signatures` (id, proposal_id, signer_name, signer_email, signed_at, ip, method).
- Pages: `/finance/proposals` (list + create), `/finance/proposals/[id]` (preview + send).
- APIs: CRUD + `/api/finance/proposals/[id]/send` (generates shareable link).
- Templates: 5 built-in scopes (SEO retainers, full-service monthly, one-time project, social media management, PPC management).
- Status flow: draft → sent → viewed → signed → accepted → expired.

### 1.3 Contracts
**Status:** Missing.
**Add:**
- DB table: `contracts` (id, workspace, proposal_id, client_id, title, body, status, start_date, end_date, auto_renew, signed_at, signature_data JSON, fee, currency, created_at).
- Auto-generate draft from accepted proposal.
- E-signature via built-in canvas capture (no third-party dependency) or integrate existing open-source signature flow.
- Pages: `/finance/contracts`, `/finance/contracts/[id]`.
- APIs: CRUD + `/api/finance/contracts/[id]/sign`.

### 1.4 Client Onboarding
**Status:** Missing.
**Add:**
- DB table: `onboarding_workflows` (id, workspace, name, steps JSON, trigger_event, created_at).
- DB table: `onboarding_runs` (id, workflow_id, client_id, current_step, status, completed_at, created_at).
- Auto-trigger when contract is signed or client is manually added.
- Steps: welcome email, questionnaire, document upload, kickoff call scheduling, first deliverable.
- Pages: `/clients/onboarding`, `/clients/onboarding/[id]`.

### 1.5 Client Messaging
**Status:** Missing unified client comms.
**Add:**
- DB table: `client_messages` (id, workspace, client_id, sender_type, sender_name, body, attachments JSON, read_at, created_at).
- Threaded per client/proposal/project.
- Real-time feel via polling + email notifications for unread.
- UI in portal + /clients/messages.

### 1.6 Automated Client Reporting
**Status:** Basic reports exist, but no scheduled per-client branded PDF delivery.
**Add:**
- DB table: `client_reports` (id, workspace, client_id, report_type, template_id, schedule_frequency, last_sent_at, next_send_at, recipients, created_at).
- PDF generation using existing `jspdf` (already in deps).
- Pull from dashboard/analytics data per client.
- Pages: `/reports/clients`, `/reports/clients/[id]`.
- APIs: `/api/reports/clients`, `/api/reports/clients/[id]/schedule`.

---

## Phase 2: Agency Operations Layer (Run the Work + Track the Money)

### 2.1 Project Management (Beyond Tasks)
**Status:** Only basic `tasks` table exists. No proper projects, milestones, or dependencies.
**Add:**
- DB table: `projects` (id, workspace, client_id, client_name, name, description, status, start_date, end_date, budget, budget_currency, manager_id, created_at).
- DB table: `milestones` (id, project_id, name, due_date, status, created_at).
- DB table: `project_files` (id, project_id, name, url, size, uploaded_by, created_at).
- Kanban + timeline views. Link tasks, timesheets, invoices, and expenses to projects.
- Pages: `/projects`, `/projects/[id]`.
- APIs: CRUD + `/api/projects/[id]/files`.

### 2.2 Retainer Management
**Status:** Missing.
**Add:**
- Extend `clients` or `projects` with retainer fields: purchased_hours, rate_per_hour, carryover_hours, period_start, period_end.
- DB table: `retainer_usage` (id, client_id, period_start, period_end, hours_purchased, hours_used, hours_carryover, amount_billed, amount_paid, created_at).
- Auto-alert when retainer hits 80% / 100%.
- Pages: `/finance/retainers`.
- APIs: `/api/finance/retainers`.

### 2.3 Rate Cards
**Status:** Missing.
**Add:**
- DB table: `rate_cards` (id, workspace, role_name, hourly_rate, currency, effective_date, created_at).
- Pages: `/finance/rate-cards`.
- Dropdown in timesheet entry to auto-fill rate from rate card.

### 2.4 Capacity Planning
**Status:** Missing.
**Add:**
- Use existing `timesheets` + new `capacity_allocations` table (person_id, week_start, hours_allocated, project_id).
- Dashboard showing utilization % per person per week.
- Pages: `/finance/capacity`.

### 2.5 Client Approvals
**Status:** `approvals` table exists but is for AI agent runs, not client deliverables.
**Add:**
- Repurpose or add `deliverable_approvals` table (id, project_id, client_id, title, description, file_url, feedback, revision_count, status, created_at).
- Clients can approve or request revision directly in portal.
- Pages: `/projects/approvals`.

---

## Phase 3: Marketing-First Enhancements

### 3.1 AI Proposal Generator
- Use existing copy-generation patterns to draft proposal bodies from `campaign_briefs` + `pricing_strategies`.
- Button inside `/finance/proposals/new`: “Generate with AI”.

### 3.2 Public Booking / Intake
**Status:** `/book` sidebar link exists but page is missing or stubbed.
**Add:**
- DB table: `booking_pages` (id, workspace, slug, title, services JSON, duration_minutes, buffer_minutes, availability_rules, created_at).
- Public pages: `/book/[slug]` with calendar + form.
- Calendly-style slot picker.

### 3.3 Reputation Management Consolidation
**Status:** `/reviews` sidebar link exists but page is missing.
**Add:**
- `/reviews` page pulling from existing `testimonials` + external review sources.
- Auto-request review after project completion.

---

## Data Model Summary (Tables to Add)
| Table | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `proposals` | Proposals with line items, status, terms |
| `proposal_signatures` | E-signatures on proposals |
| `contracts` | Contracts linked to proposals/clients |
| `onboarding_workflows` | Reusable onboarding step templates |
| `onboarding_runs` | Per-client onboarding execution |
| `client_messages` | Threaded client ↔ agency messaging |
| `client_reports` | Scheduled per-client report configs |
| `projects` | Proper project records (not just tasks) |
| `milestones` | Project milestones |
| `project_files` | Per-project shared documents |
| `retainer_usage` | Retainer hour tracking |
| `rate_cards` | Billable rates per role |
| `capacity_allocations` | Team capacity / utilization |
| `booking_pages` | Public intake/booking pages |
| `deliverable_approvals` | Client proofing & approval |

---

## Migration / Rollout Path
1. **Sprint 1 (Week 1):** Proposals + E-signature + Contracts. Unlock the quote-to-cash flow.
2. **Sprint 2 (Week 2):** Client Portal upgrade + Client Messaging. Make the experience premium.
3. **Sprint 3 (Week 3):** Project Management (projects, milestones, files) + Retainer + Rate Cards.
4. **Sprint 4 (Week 4):** Client Onboarding + Automated Reporting + Capacity Planning.
5. **Sprint 5 (Week 5):** Booking/Intake + Reputation + AI proposal generator + polish.

Auto-migrations: add `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` blocks into `src/lib/db/index.ts` inside `init()`.

Validation: every new page returns 200; every new API supports GET (list), GET [id] (detail), POST (create); checkout that `/api/finance/proposals` and `/api/finance/contracts` round-trip.

---

## Risks & Notes
- **Do not replace existing marketing execution tools.** Keep CRM, campaigns, SEO, ads, etc. intact.
- **Reuse existing patterns:** `useFetch`, `Modal`, `apiFetch`, `jspdf`, `lucide-react`, card/section layouts, sidebar nav group.
- **Keep the app offline-first / local-first:** new tables are pure SQLite. No external APIs required for core flows.
- **Billing vs Finance:** existing `/billing` is for platform subscriptions (Stripe). New `/finance/invoices` and `/finance/proposals` are for client billing. Do not conflate them.

+- Dropdown in timesheet entry to auto-fill rate from rate card.
+
+### 2.4 Capacity Planning
+**Status:** Missing.
+**Add:**
+- Use existing `timesheets` + new `capacity_allocations` table (person_id, week_start, hours_allocated, project_id).
+- Dashboard showing utilization % per person per week.
+- Pages: `/finance/capacity`.
+
+### 2.5 Client Approvals
+**Status:** `approvals` table exists but is for AI agent runs, not client deliverables.
+**Add:**
+- Repurpose or add `deliverable_approvals` table (id, project_id, client_id, title, description, file_url, feedback, revision_count, status, created_at).
+- Clients can approve or request revision directly in portal.
+- Pages: `/projects/approvals`.
+
+---
+
+## Phase 3: Marketing-First Enhancements
+
+### 3.1 AI Proposal Generator
+- Use existing copy-generation patterns to draft proposal bodies from `campaign_briefs` + `pricing_strategies`.
+- Button inside `/finance/proposals/new`: “Generate with AI”.
+
+### 3.2 Public Booking / Intake
+**Status:** `/book` sidebar link exists but page is missing or stubbed.
+**Add:**
+- DB table: `booking_pages` (id, workspace, slug, title, services JSON, duration_minutes, buffer_minutes, availability_rules, created_at).
+- Public pages: `/book/[slug]` with calendar + form.
+- Calendly-style slot picker.
+
+### 3.3 Reputation Management Consolidation
+**Status:** `/reviews` sidebar link exists but page is missing.
+**Add:**
+- `/reviews` page pulling from existing `testimonials` + external review sources.
+- Auto-request review after project completion.
+
+---
+
+## Data Model Summary (Tables to Add)
+| Table | Purpose |
+|---|---|
+| `proposals` | Proposals with line items, status, terms |
+| `proposal_signatures` | E-signatures on proposals |
+| `contracts` | Contracts linked to proposals/clients |
+| `onboarding_workflows` | Reusable onboarding step templates |
+| `onboarding_runs` | Per-client onboarding execution |
+| `client_messages` | Threaded client ↔ agency messaging |
+| `client_reports` | Scheduled per-client report configs |
+| `projects` | Proper project records (not just tasks) |
+| `milestones` | Project milestones |
+| `project_files` | Per-project shared documents |
+| `retainer_usage` | Retainer hour tracking |
+| `rate_cards` | Billable rates per role |
+| `capacity_allocations` | Team capacity / utilization |
+| `booking_pages` | Public intake/booking pages |
+| `deliverable_approvals` | Client proofing & approval |
+
+---
+
+## Migration / Rollout Path
+1. **Sprint 1 (Week 1):** Proposals + E-signature + Contracts. Unlock the quote-to-cash flow.
+2. **Sprint 2 (Week 2):** Client Portal upgrade + Client Messaging. Make the experience premium.
+3. **Sprint 3 (Week 3):** Project Management (projects, milestones, files) + Retainer + Rate Cards.
+4. **Sprint 4 (Week 4):** Client Onboarding + Automated Reporting + Capacity Planning.
+5. **Sprint 5 (Week 5):** Booking/Intake + Reputation + AI proposal generator + polish.
+
+Auto-migrations: add `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` blocks into `src/lib/db/index.ts` inside `init()`.
+
+Validation: every new page returns 200; every new API supports GET (list), GET [id] (detail), POST (create); checkout that `/api/finance/proposals` and `/api/finance/contracts` round-trip.
+
+---
+
+## Risks & Notes
+- **Do not replace existing marketing execution tools.** Keep CRM, campaigns, SEO, ads, etc. intact.
+- **Reuse existing patterns:** `useFetch`, `Modal`, `apiFetch`, `jspdf`, `lucide-react`, card/section layouts, sidebar nav group.
+- **Keep the app offline-first / local-first:** new tables are pure SQLite. No external APIs required for core flows.
+- **Billing vs Finance:** existing `/billing` is for platform subscriptions (Stripe). New `/finance/invoices` and `/finance/proposals` are for client billing. Do not conflate them.

+# Marketing Hub — Agency Completeness Plan
+
+**Goal:** Ship every feature a professional marketing agency needs to run client work, bill accurately, and deliver a premium client experience — all inside this Next.js + SQLite hub.
+
+**Context:**
+- `marketing-hub` (private workspace) already has 90+ DB tables, 120+ API routes, 44+ pages.
+- Core marketing execution is solid: SEO, ads, CRM, campaigns, email, landing pages, funnels, analytics, automations, A/B tests, inbox, surveys, reports, copy generator.
+- Agency/finance layer is partially built: **timesheets**, **client invoices**, **project PnL**, **contractors**, **payouts**, **billing**, **portal links**, **agency client accounts**, **approvals**.
+- What’s missing is the **client-facing experience layer** and **agency operations depth** that top platforms (Kobin AI, AgencyPro, SuiteDash, Assembly, Scoro) already ship.
+
+---
+
+## Phase 1: Client Experience Layer (The “Agency” in Marketing Agency)
+
+### 1.1 White-Label Client Portal
+**Status:** `portal_links` table + `/api/portal-links` exists, but `/c/[token]` is a minimal read-only shell.
+**Add:**
+- Dedicated portal pages: `/portal/[token]` with full dashboard showing project status, invoices, files, upcoming events, and messages.
+- Workspace-level white-label settings in Settings: custom domain, logo, primary color, email sender name.
+- Secure, token-scoped data access — clients see only their own data.
+- “Request support” / message thread inside portal.
+
+### 1.2 Proposals + E-Signature
+**Status:** Completely missing.
+**Add:**
+- DB table: `proposals` (id, workspace, client_id, title, line_items JSON, total, currency, status, due_date, terms, cover_letter, template_id, created_at).
+- DB table: `proposal_signatures` (id, proposal_id, signer_name, signer_email, signed_at, ip, method).
+- Pages: `/finance/proposals` (list + create), `/finance/proposals/[id]` (preview + send).
+- APIs: CRUD + `/api/finance/proposals/[id]/send` (generates shareable link).
+- Templates: 5 built-in scopes (SEO retainers, full-service monthly, one-time project, social media management, PPC management).
+- Status flow: draft → sent → viewed → signed → accepted → expired.
+
+### 1.3 Contracts
+**Status:** Missing.
+**Add:**
+- DB table: `contracts` (id, workspace, proposal_id, client_id, title, body, status, start_date, end_date, auto_renew, signed_at, signature_data JSON, fee, currency, created_at).
+- Auto-generate draft from accepted proposal.
+- E-signature via built-in canvas capture (no third-party dependency) or integrate existing open-source signature flow.
+- Pages: `/finance/contracts`, `/finance/contracts/[id]`.
+- APIs: CRUD + `/api/finance/contracts/[id]/sign`.
+
+### 1.4 Client Onboarding
+**Status:** Missing.
+**Add:**
+- DB table: `onboarding_workflows` (id, workspace, name, steps JSON, trigger_event, created_at).
+- DB table: `onboarding_runs` (id, workflow_id, client_id, current_step, status, completed_at, created_at).
+- Auto-trigger when contract is signed or client is manually added.
+- Steps: welcome email, questionnaire, document upload, kickoff call scheduling, first deliverable.
+- Pages: `/clients/onboarding`, `/clients/onboarding/[id]`.
+
+### 1.5 Client Messaging
+**Status:** Missing unified client comms.
+**Add:**
+- DB table: `client_messages` (id, workspace, client_id, sender_type, sender_name, body, attachments JSON, read_at, created_at).
+- Threaded per client/proposal/project.
+- Real-time feel via polling + email notifications for unread.
+- UI in portal + /clients/messages.
+
+### 1.6 Automated Client Reporting
+**Status:** Basic reports exist, but no scheduled per-client branded PDF delivery.
+**Add:**
+- DB table: `client_reports` (id, workspace, client_id, report_type, template_id, schedule_frequency, last_sent_at, next_send_at, recipients, created_at).
+- PDF generation using existing `jspdf` (already in deps).
+- Pull from dashboard/analytics data per client.
+- Pages: `/reports/clients`, `/reports/clients/[id]`.
+- APIs: `/api/reports/clients`, `/api/reports/clients/[id]/schedule`.
+
+---
+
+## Phase 2: Agency Operations Layer (Run the Work + Track the Money)
+
+### 2.1 Project Management (Beyond Tasks)
+**Status:** Only basic `tasks` table exists. No proper projects, milestones, or dependencies.
+**Add:**
+- DB table: `projects` (id, workspace, client_id, client_name, name, description, status, start_date, end_date, budget, budget_currency, manager_id, created_at).
+- DB table: `milestones` (id, project_id, name, due_date, status, created_at).
+- DB table: `project_files` (id, project_id, name, url, size, uploaded_by, created_at).
+- Kanban + timeline views. Link tasks, timesheets, invoices, and expenses to projects.
+- Pages: `/projects`, `/projects/[id]`.
+- APIs: CRUD + `/api/projects/[id]/files`.
+
+### 2.2 Retainer Management
+**Status:** Missing.
+**Add:**
+- Extend `clients` or `projects` with retainer fields: purchased_hours, rate_per_hour, carryover_hours, period_start, period_end.
+- DB table: `retainer_usage` (id, client_id, period_start, period_end, hours_purchased, hours_used, hours_carryover, amount_billed, amount_paid, created_at).
+- Auto-alert when retainer hits 80% / 100%.
+- Pages: `/finance/retainers`.
+- APIs: `/api/finance/retainers`.
+
+### 2.3 Rate Cards
+**Status:** Missing.
+**Add:**
+- DB table: `rate_cards` (id, workspace, role_name, hourly_rate, currency, effective_date, created_at).
+- Pages: `/finance/rate-cards`.
+- Dropdown in timesheet entry to auto-fill rate from rate card.
+
+### 2.4 Capacity Planning
+**Status:** Missing.
+**Add:**
+- Use existing `timesheets` + new `capacity_allocations` table (person_id, week_start, hours_allocated, project_id).
+- Dashboard showing utilization % per person per week.
+- Pages: `/finance/capacity`.
+
+### 2.5 Client Approvals
+**Status:** `approvals` table exists but is for AI agent runs, not client deliverables.
+**Add:**
+- Repurpose or add `deliverable_approvals` table (id, project_id, client_id, title, description, file_url, feedback, revision_count, status, created_at).
+- Clients can approve or request revision directly in portal.
+- Pages: `/projects/approvals`.
+
+---
+
+## Phase 3: Marketing-First Enhancements
+
+### 3.1 AI Proposal Generator
+- Use existing copy-generation patterns to draft proposal bodies from `campaign_briefs` + `pricing_strategies`.
+- Button inside `/finance/proposals/new`: “Generate with AI”.
+
+### 3.2 Public Booking / Intake
+**Status:** `/book` sidebar link exists but page is missing or stubbed.
+**Add:**
+- DB table: `booking_pages` (id, workspace, slug, title, services JSON, duration_minutes, buffer_minutes, availability_rules, created_at).
+- Public pages: `/book/[slug]` with calendar + form.
+- Calendly-style slot picker.
+
+### 3.3 Reputation Management Consolidation
+**Status:** `/reviews` sidebar link exists but page is missing.
+**Add:**
+- `/reviews` page pulling from existing `testimonials` + external review sources.
+- Auto-request review after project completion.
+
+---
+
+## Data Model Summary (Tables to Add)
+| Table | Purpose |
+|---|---|
+| `proposals` | Proposals with line items, status, terms |
+| `proposal_signatures` | E-signatures on proposals |
+| `contracts` | Contracts linked to proposals/clients |
+| `onboarding_workflows` | Reusable onboarding step templates |
+| `onboarding_runs` | Per-client onboarding execution |
+| `client_messages` | Threaded client ↔ agency messaging |
+| `client_reports` | Scheduled per-client report configs |
+| `projects` | Proper project records (not just tasks) |
+| `milestones` | Project milestones |
+| `project_files` | Per-project shared documents |
+| `retainer_usage` | Retainer hour tracking |
+| `rate_cards` | Billable rates per role |
+| `capacity_allocations` | Team capacity / utilization |
+| `booking_pages` | Public intake/booking pages |
+| `deliverable_approvals` | Client proofing & approval |
+
+---
+
+## Migration / Rollout Path
+1. **Sprint 1 (Week 1):** Proposals + E-signature + Contracts. Unlock the quote-to-cash flow.
+2. **Sprint 2 (Week 2):** Client Portal upgrade + Client Messaging. Make the experience premium.
+3. **Sprint 3 (Week 3):** Project Management (projects, milestones, files) + Retainer + Rate Cards.
+4. **Sprint 4 (Week 4):** Client Onboarding + Automated Reporting + Capacity Planning.
+5. **Sprint 5 (Week 5):** Booking/Intake + Reputation + AI proposal generator + polish.
+
+Auto-migrations: add `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` blocks into `src/lib/db/index.ts` inside `init()`.
+
+Validation: every new page returns 200; every new API supports GET (list), GET [id] (detail), POST (create); checkout that `/api/finance/proposals` and `/api/finance/contracts` round-trip.
+
+---
+
+## Risks & Notes
+- **Do not replace existing marketing execution tools.** Keep CRM, campaigns, SEO, ads, etc. intact.
+- **Reuse existing patterns:** `useFetch`, `Modal`, `apiFetch`, `jspdf`, `lucide-react`, card/section layouts, sidebar nav group.
+- **Keep the app offline-first / local-first:** new tables are pure SQLite. No external APIs required for core flows.
+- **Billing vs Finance:** existing `/billing` is for platform subscriptions (Stripe). New `/finance/invoices` and `/finance/proposals` are for client billing. Do not conflate them.

The biggest unresolved issue I see: Should the client portal and agency features live in `marketing-hub` (private workspace) or `marketing-hub-public` (public-facing)? This affects routing, auth, deployment, and data boundaries. I should ask this first.

Actually, looking more carefully at the codebase:
- `marketing-hub` is the private workspace app (port 3000)
- `marketing-hub-public` is the public-facing app
- The sidebar in `marketing-hub` already has `/agency` and `/roi-marketing` links
- There's already a `portal-links` API that generates `/c/[token]` URLs

But I haven't checked if `/c/[token]` is served from which app. Let me check that.

Actually I couldn't check because of permission issues. But I can infer from the code structure.

The most important question is: Should the white-label client portal and agency operations remain in `marketing-hub` or move to `marketing-hub-public`? Or should they be unified?

My recommended answer: Keep the agency operations (proposals, contracts, projects, timesheets, invoices, profitability) in `marketing-hub` as a private workspace. The client portal (`/c/[token]`) and public booking pages (`/book/[slug]`) should be served from `marketing-hub-public` and call APIs on `marketing-hub` via internal routes or a shared API layer.

But wait - is there even a `/c/[token]` route defined? The API creates tokens but I haven't verified the route exists.

Let me ask the user about the app boundary question since that's the most critical architectural decision.
